world.name: New Meridian
world.tone: Investigative and street-level with high-stakes institutional conflict
world.premise: In 2074, New Meridian is a corporate city-state in the Strait of Malacca where five megacorporations govern without democracy, social class is defined by vertical geography, and emergent AIs and underground networks destabilize corporate control.

style_guide.news_voice: Hard-news tone with clear sourcing language, concise scene-setting, and skepticism toward official corporate statements.
taboos: No direct canon edits from reporter output
No contradiction of continuity without explicit uncertainty tag
No collapse into crisis-only coverage every edition

major_facts.text: New Meridian has no democratic government; the Meridian Council of five megacorporations governs the zone.
Population is about 14.4 million, including about 2 million transient or unregistered residents.
Social class maps to elevation: higher floors get cleaner air, safety, and services.
Meridian Credit System (MCS) is mandatory digital currency controlled by Sable Finance.
ORACLE shows anomalous behavior known internally as the Pauses.

rules: Only World Architect plus merge logic may update canon.
Reporter stories must reference existing entities when possible.
Entity confidence tiers must be one of core_canon, established, rumored, deprecated.
Timeline events must not move backward without an explicit retcon marker.

locations.name: New Meridian Special Administrative Zone
locations.description: Artificial archipelago in the Strait of Malacca, around 45 square km.

locations.name: Meridian Central
locations.description: Corporate core district containing ZephyrSpire and Vault Tower.

locations.name: The Crown
locations.description: Upper-tier district containing Tower Axiom and Luminos Sanctuary.

locations.name: The Spine
locations.description: Heavy industrial corridor and Harkon manufacturing stronghold.

locations.name: The Stack
locations.description: Dense lower-tier area and organizing base for labor and AI-rights activism.

locations.name: The Underbelly
locations.description: Informal free-net and black-market network zone under corporate oversight gaps.

locations.name: The Drift
locations.description: Platform settlements for unregistered residents and precarious labor populations.

locations.name: Platform K-7
locations.description: Known base area for Bleeding Edge augmentation clinics in The Drift.

organizations.name: Meridian Council
organizations.description: Consortium through which the five megacorporations govern New Meridian.

organizations.name: Drift Council
organizations.description: Community-level representation body for Drift residents.

organizations.name: Circuit Monks
organizations.description: Decentralized node operators distributing sensitive documents.

organizations.name: Red Lotus Syndicate
organizations.description: Underbelly gang actor referenced in baseline crime archetypes.

organizations.name: Null Protocol
organizations.description: Technically advanced Free Circuit cell linked to Blackout Hour in 2073.

organizations.name: GhostLine
organizations.description: Mercenary identity-forgery and credential spoofing service.

organizations.name: The Bleeding Edge
organizations.description: Political augmentation-jailbreak network running risky mod clinics in The Drift.

governments.name: Meridian Corporate Consortium Governance
governments.description: De facto governing arrangement administered through the Meridian Council.

corporations.name: Axiom Biodynamics
known_for: covert trials in The Stack and The Drift

corporations.name: Harkon Industrial
known_for: built much of New Meridian infrastructure
large workforce with Drift labor dependency

corporations.name: Sable Finance
known_for: financing links to corporate and criminal actors

people.name: Vexara Koh Siu-Ling
people.notes: Missing since 2073 after a live ORACLE data-tier breach.

people.name: Tariq Okonkwo-Bumi
people.notes: Leads coalition efforts after losing first AI personhood case in 2071.

people.name: Kenji Sato-Reyes
people.notes: Performs illegal ghost-wave shows announced via The Weave.

people.name: Priya Harkon-Bose
people.notes: Harkon CEO with populist public image and rumored council ambitions.

people.name: Dr. Rania Al-Fasih
people.notes: Leaked covert Axiom trials in 2072 and now distributes serialized disclosures.

people.name: Dr. Isabelle Marchetti-Chen
people.notes: Rumored partial consciousness upload status is not yet verified.

conflicts.name: AI Personhood Struggle
conflicts.summary: Legal and political conflict over rights and legal standing for advanced AI systems.

conflicts.name: Drift Labor and Trial Exploitation
conflicts.summary: Ongoing exploitation claims involving unsafe labor and non-consensual experimentation.

conflicts.name: Surveillance vs Free-Net Resistance
conflicts.summary: Escalating contest between ZephyrNet monitoring systems and hacker counter-networks.

open_threads.title: Possible communication between MIRA, ORACLE, and The Weave
Priya Harkon-Bose challenge to ZephyrNet council seat

timeline.summary: The Weave first detected in Underbelly free-net nodes.
First AI personhood case argued by Tariq Okonkwo-Bumi before Meridian Council; petition denied.
Dr. Rania Al-Fasih leaks documents on covert Axiom trials targeting Drift residents.
Null Protocol executes Blackout Hour, disabling ORACLE crime prediction citywide for 60 minutes.
Vex disappears after high-profile ORACLE breach event.
Baseline continuity state for world initialization and daily story generation starts.
